food_subgroup: Fast food burger meal set (burger + fries + drink)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general hamburger class (FOODON_00002383) but no specific entry for Jollibee Deluxe Jolliburger or a branded fast food burger meal set. closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers hamburger as a generic category. No specific code exists for a branded Jollibee burger meal set. closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for a branded fast food burger meal set.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Jollibee Deluxe Jolliburger meal set consisting of a single-patty beef burger, a regular portion of french fries, and a regular-sized carbonated soft drink, estimated at approximately 650 g total.

nutrient_source.title: Fast Foods, hamburger; double, large patty; with condiments and vegetables — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy adapted to single-patty Jollibee Deluxe Jolliburger meal set
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact nutritional entry exists for the Jollibee Deluxe Jolliburger Meal (Single Patty) in any approved primary source. Values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for: (1) fast food hamburger single patty with condiments and vegetables (USDA FDC ID 21113), (2) fast food french fries (USDA FDC ID 21138), and (3) a regular cola soft drink (USDA FDC ID 14400), weighted by estimated component proportions for a standard Jollibee meal set (burger ~200 g, fries ~115 g, drink ~335 g / ~140 g beverage contribution to weight). Per-100g values are derived from the blended composite. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Hamburger bun and fast food burger composite GI — analogous estimate based on white bread GI reference and fast food burger meal data from the University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for the Jollibee Deluxe Jolliburger Meal. GI of 66 is estimated as a weighted composite: hamburger bun (GI ~70, medium, University of Sydney GI Database), french fries (GI ~63, medium, University of Sydney GI Database), and cola soft drink (GI ~63, medium, University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted by estimated carbohydrate contribution per component. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (66 × 159.25) / 100 ≈ 104 g GL, where available carbohydrate per 650 g serving = 24.5 × 6.5 = 159.25 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~310 mg per 100 g; ~2015 mg per meal set)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal set due to its very high sodium content (~2015 mg per serving), which represents approximately 87% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg; frequent consumption is strongly discouraged.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~104 per meal set) and high refined carbohydrate content from bun, fries, and cola
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this meal set due to its very high glycemic load (~104 per serving) driven by the refined-carbohydrate burger bun, french fries, and sugar-sweetened cola, all of which cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~2.9 g per 100 g; ~18.9 g per meal), trans fat (~0.2 g per 100 g), and high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this meal set due to its combination of saturated fat (~18.9 g per serving), trans fat, and very high sodium, all of which are independently associated with increased cardiovascular risk per AHA dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Deluxe Jolliburger Meal (Single Patty) with Drink is a fast food burger meal set sold by Jollibee, a Filipino-origin quick-service restaurant chain with outlets in Singapore including Jollibee Bugis Village. The meal consists of a single-patty beef burger (the Deluxe Jolliburger) served with a regular portion of french fries and a regular-sized carbonated soft drink. The Deluxe Jolliburger features a seasoned beef patty on a soft sesame-seed bun, layered with fresh lettuce, tomato slices, onion, mayonnaise, ketchup, and a slice of processed cheese, giving it a savoury, slightly sweet flavour profile characteristic of Jollibee's Filipino-American fast food style. The fries are thin-cut, salted, and deep-fried. The meal is classified as ultra-processed and is high in refined carbohydrates, saturated fat, sodium, and total calories. Per 100 g of the composite meal, it provides approximately 195 kcal, 7.2 g protein, 8.1 g fat (2.9 g saturated), and 24.5 g carbohydrate. A full meal set (~650 g) delivers approximately 1268 kcal, making it a high-calorie single-meal option. The glycemic index of the composite is estimated at 66 (medium), but the glycemic load per serving is very high (~104), posing significant concern for blood glucose management in individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The meal is certified Halal at Jollibee Singapore outlets. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those on low-sodium or low-glycaemic diets. Clinically, this meal is of high concern for hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease due to its sodium, saturated fat, trans fat, and high glycaemic load.

Is the Jollibee Deluxe Jolliburger Halal in Singapore?
Jollibee burger meal nutrition facts Singapore

Fast food burger meal glycemic index

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for the Jollibee Deluxe Jolliburger Meal (Single Patty) exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central branded, or ICMR-NIN). Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component entries for fast food hamburger (single patty with condiments), fast food french fries, and cola soft drink, blended by estimated component weight proportions. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for hamburger bun, french fries, and cola. Confidence is medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component entries (fast food hamburger single patty, french fries, cola) blended by estimated component weight proportions (burger ~200 g, fries ~115 g, drink ~335 g total / ~140 g beverage weight contribution). Actual Jollibee product formulation may differ. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy fast food component entries used for this estimation. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy fast food component entries used for this estimation. 3. Trans fat is present at a low level (~0.2 g per 100 g) consistent with USDA SR Legacy data for fast food beef patties; this is not zero and is clinically relevant for cardiovascular risk. 4. The meal is tagged Halal based on Jollibee Singapore's Halal certification status as reported on the Jollibee Singapore brand communications; this should be verified against the current MUIS Halal certificate. 5. Serving size of 650 g is an estimate for a standard D-size (regular) meal set; actual weights may vary by outlet. 6. GI of 66 and GL of ~104 per serving are analogous estimates; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 7. FoodOn URI http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/FOODON_00002383 and FoodEx2 code A0CKP are used as closest_parent matches for hamburger; no branded fast food meal set code exists in either ontology.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed full multilingual local_names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi as required for Singapore-served fast food cuisine.
Estimated per_100g nutrient values from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component entries (fast food hamburger single patty, french fries, cola) with documented blending methodology.
Documented -1 sentinel values for vitamin_d_mcg and selenium_mcg with explicit source-gap justification in notes.
Estimated GI of 66 (medium) as weighted composite from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for hamburger bun, french fries, and cola; GL per serving calculated at 104.
Selected FoodOn FOODON_00002383 and FoodEx2 A0CKP as closest_parent matches with gap notes; SNOMED CT set to null with none match_type and gap note.
Wrote health context for Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and Cardiovascular Disease with specific clinical guidance.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency — all language-specific alias strings appear verbatim in flat aliases list.
Confirmed no zero core macros requiring zero-justification phrases.
Set confidence to medium with full justification.
